name: Rogue Device Detection & Isolation
description: Detects and responds to unauthorized devices discovered on the network through NAC alerts or MAC address anomalies. Riggs AI analyzes the device fingerprint, MAC vendor lookup, and connection context to determine if the device is rogue or a legitimate unregistered asset. Confirmed rogue devices trigger switch port isolation, MAC address blocking, a security alert to the SOC, and an incident ticket. Legitimate devices are flagged for IT asset registration.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Cisco ISE
required_integrations.reason: Isolate rogue device by disabling the switch port and blocking the MAC address

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create incident ticket for physical security investigation of rogue device

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Alert SOC and physical security teams about the rogue device

config.description: Triggers when NAC or network monitoring detects an unknown or unauthorized device connecting to the network

config.additional_instructions: Analyze device fingerprint: MAC vendor lookup (OUI), DHCP fingerprint, OS detection, connected switch/port, and VLAN. Cross-reference against CMDB/asset inventory. Determine if this is a rogue device or a legitimate unregistered asset.
config.description: AI-powered device fingerprinting and asset inventory cross-reference to classify the unknown device as rogue or legitimate
Routes based on whether the device is confirmed rogue versus a legitimate unregistered asset

parameters.comment: Port disabled — rogue device detected. MAC: {{trigger_1.alert.mac_address}}, Vendor: {{analyze_1.mac_vendor}}

config.description: Disables the switch port where the rogue device is connected to immediately sever network access
Adds the rogue device MAC address to the NAC blacklist to prevent it from reconnecting on any port
Creates an incident ticket for physical security team to investigate and remove the rogue device
Notifies the SOC and physical security teams about the rogue device with location details

parameters.note:
Unknown device analyzed — Verdict: {{analyze_1.verdict}}. Device appears legitimate but unregistered.
MAC: {{trigger_1.alert.mac_address}} ({{analyze_1.mac_vendor}})
Type: {{analyze_1.device_type}}

Routed to IT for asset registration and CMDB update.

config.description: Routes the alert to the IT asset team for proper registration when the device is legitimate but not in the CMDB
Rogue device detection and isolation workflow complete
